annotations.label: Monodromy (informal)

anchor.snippet: monodromy is the study of how objects from mathematical analysis

annotations.note: Mathlib formalises the covering-space instance of monodromy via `IsCoveringMap.monodromy`, but not the informal umbrella concept spanning analysis/algebra/geometry.

annotations.label: Monodromy group (informal)

anchor.snippet: The failure of monodromy can be measured by defining a monodromy group

annotations.note: The `monodromyPerm` homomorphism packages the fundamental-group action as permutations, whose image is the covering-space monodromy group; no general 'monodromy group' abstraction exists.

annotations.label: Base fiber of a covering

anchor.snippet: Define its base fiber

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the raw preimage `p ⁻¹' {x}` (as in the codomain of `IsCoveringMap.monodromy`) rather than a named 'base fiber' abstraction.

annotations.label: Lift of a loop through a covering

anchor.snippet: can be lifted through the covering map

annotations.note: `IsCoveringMap.liftPath` lifts any path (hence any loop) given a lift of the starting point, with `liftPath_lifts`/`liftPath_zero` witnessing the covering property.

annotations.label: Well-definedness of monodromy action on homotopy classes

anchor.snippet: the covering homotopy property shows they define the same action

annotations.note: Lifting two paths that are homotopic rel `{0,1}` from the same start-point yields lifts with the same endpoint, exactly the well-definedness invoked by `IsCoveringMap.monodromy` on `Path.Homotopic.Quotient`.

annotations.label: Monodromy action on the base fiber

anchor.snippet: This is the monodromy action on

annotations.note: `IsCoveringMap.fundamentalGroupMulAction` supplies the `MulAction (FundamentalGroup X x) (p ⁻¹' {x})` directly from `IsCoveringMap.monodromy`.

annotations.label: Stabilizer of a point under monodromy

anchor.snippet: The stabilizer of

annotations.note: For quotient covering maps Mathlib identifies `ker (monodromyPerm x) = range (mapOfEq p e.2)`; the general 'stabilizer of a fiber point equals image of `π₁(Y,ỹ)`' statement is not stated for arbitrary covers.

annotations.label: Topological monodromy group

anchor.snippet: is the topological monodromy group

annotations.note: The image of `monodromyPerm x` is the topological monodromy group, but Mathlib does not name it as a distinct subgroup.

annotations.label: Algebraic monodromy group

anchor.snippet: is the algebraic monodromy

annotations.note: No Zariski-closure/algebraic-group notion of a monodromy representation exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Analytic continuation around a puncture (logarithm)

anchor.snippet: analytic continuation anti-clockwise round the circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Complex.log` and continuation lemmas for L-series but not this monodromy-of-logarithm example.

annotations.label: Monodromy group of the logarithm is infinite cyclic

anchor.snippet: In this case the monodromy group is the infinite cyclic group

annotations.note: The specific computation π₁(ℂ\{0}) ≅ ℤ acting by shifts on logarithm branches is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Universal cover of the punctured plane as helicoid

anchor.snippet: This cover can be visualized as the helicoid with parametric equations

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no `helicoid` construction nor an explicit universal cover of the punctured plane.

annotations.label: Monodromy group of a linear differential equation

anchor.section: Differential equations in the complex domain
anchor.snippet: Linear differential equations defined in an open, connected set

annotations.note: Mathlib does not develop the monodromy representation attached to solutions of a linear ODE on a complex domain.

annotations.label: Riemann–Hilbert problem (inverse monodromy)

anchor.section: Differential equations in the complex domain
anchor.snippet: is a Riemann–Hilbert problem

annotations.note: No `RiemannHilbert` or inverse-monodromy notion is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generator relation for Fuchsian monodromy

anchor.section: Differential equations in the complex domain
anchor.snippet: the only relation between the generators is the equality

annotations.note: The Fuchsian relation M₁···Mₙ = 1 among loop monodromies is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deligne–Simpson realisation problem

anchor.section: Differential equations in the complex domain
anchor.snippet: The Deligne–Simpson problem is the following realisation problem

annotations.note: The Deligne–Simpson realisability problem is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monodromy as permutation action on the fiber

anchor.snippet: to code this one considers the action of the fundamental group

annotations.note: `IsCoveringMap.monodromyPerm x : FundamentalGroup X x →* Equiv.Perm (p ⁻¹' {x})` is exactly the permutation-action encoding.

annotations.label: Holonomy group from parallel transport

anchor.snippet: to define a holonomy group of translations of the fiber at

annotations.note: Grep for `olonomy` in `Mathlib/` returned no files — Mathlib has no holonomy/parallel-transport formalisation.

annotations.label: Monodromy groupoid

anchor.snippet: to define a monodromy groupoid

annotations.note: `monodromyFunctor : FundamentalGroupoid X ⥤ Type _` records the covering's monodromy as a groupoid representation but is not itself the monodromy groupoid as an object.

annotations.label: Monodromy of a foliation via germs

anchor.snippet: the construction can also be generalized to foliations

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geometric-foliation library (only a fleeting mention in `LieRinehartAlgebra`), so germ-based foliation monodromy is absent.

annotations.label: Field of rational functions and finite extension

anchor.snippet: determines a finite field extension

annotations.note: Mathlib has `RatFunc` and general finite field extensions but no packaged 'function field of a branched cover → finite extension' construction.

annotations.label: Monodromy group via Galois closure

anchor.snippet: is called the monodromy group of

annotations.note: General Galois-theoretic monodromy of a branched cover (as `Gal(L̃/K(X))`) is not stated in Mathlib, though `Gal` and normal closure are available.

annotations.label: Group of deck transformations

anchor.snippet: the associated monodromy group is sometimes called a group of deck transformations

annotations.note: Grep for `deck`/`DeckTransformation` in `Mathlib/` shows no covering-space deck-transformation group.

annotations.label: Riemann existence theorem (connection)

anchor.snippet: leading to the Riemann existence theorem

annotations.note: The Riemann existence theorem linking finite étale covers of a Riemann surface and finite extensions of its function field is not in Mathlib.
